events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario describes a pneumatic pressure test being conducted without an exclusion zone. This is a serious safety violation. ASME B31.3 §345 requires proper safety precautions during pressure testing, especially pneumatic tests which store significant compressed energy. An exclusion zone is a critical safety requirement for pneumatic testing.

Key findings:
- Pneumatic test in progress without exclusion zone = critical safety hazard
- ASME B31.3 §345.5 covers pneumatic testing requirements including safety precautions
- Work must stop immediately, exclusion zone must be established before continuing

Decision: fail (dangerous/non-compliant)
Risk: critical
Work stage: in-progress
Component conditions: non-compliant (the test procedure/setup is non-compliant)
Percent complete: estimate maybe 50% since test is in progress
Confidence: high, 0.95

    "Stop the pneumatic test immediately and depressurize the system",
    "Establish a barricaded exclusion zone with controlled access and warning signage before any re-pressurization",
    "Verify test procedure and permit address pneumatic test safety precautions per ASME B31.3 §345.5",
    "Confirm all non-essential personnel are cleared from the exclusion zone before resuming test"
  ],
  "rationale": "A pneumatic pressure test without an exclusion zone is a critical safety violation. ASME B31.3 §345 requires that pneumatic tests include provisions for personnel safety due to the stored energy hazard. Work cannot continue until the exclusion zone is established and the test procedure is verified compliant.",
